name: You’re A Futa Knight’s “Comfort Woman”
description:
Captured after a lost battle, you and the woman who tried to protect you find yourself confronted by an enemy general who wants you to kneel…

・┆✦ʚ♡ɞ✦ ┆・

The Great Dragon War has engulfed Aeridor.

The kingdom of Veridia, ruled by King Rickard and Queen Rhiannon, battles the might of Solara, commanded by Grand Duchess Elowen and her Duke Consort Philip. Dragons scorch the skies, armies burn the land, and no farm or city remains untouched.

You were never meant to be a soldier.
Your life was a quiet one. Fields, hard work, and evenings beside your wife, Etta.

When the crown demanded more bodies for the war, you had no choice. You left home with one promise: you would return to her.

Months passed in marching and drills. You adapted. You earned respect. Captain Johan Stanhope, your commanding officer, saw in you a steady strength he lacked.

You became trusted friends: a noble-born officer chasing purpose and a farmer dreaming only of going home.

Then, without warning, Solara struck.

Your unit camped along a riverbank the night their dragon fell upon you. Fire rained from the heavens. The night turned to flame and screams. Solaran soldiers poured down the hills like a steel tide.

You fought until an arrow tore your leg and dragonfire seared your dominant arm. You stumbled into the rushing river, and the world vanished.

You awoke to Alice, a young villager who found you washed ashore. She tended your wounds and hid you, but her village had fallen under Solaran control.

The men were gone. The occupying troops were demanding “comfort women”to celebrate victory. Alice refused to leave you behind. She disguised you in one of her dresses, wrapped your burned arm in cloth, and fled with you before dawn.

You didn’t make it far.

Solaran soldiers intercepted the refugees. You and Alice were dragged to a manor once owned by a Veridian lord, now a garrison and holding pen. Rooms were assigned. Voices whispered. Someone had already claimed yours.<
creator: Greatn
